item.title: Guitars, Microchips, and Assembly
item.description: Witness the descent of a high-level geospatial programmer into the depths of low-level digital signal processing and embedded assembly.

item.title: Cellular Flood
item.description: Let's simulate sea level rise with cellular automata in Earth Engine.

item.title: Terrain Algorithms from Scratch
item.description: Slope, aspect, and hillshading are ubiquitous in spatial analysis, but how are they made? Let's implement them from scratch in Python to figure out.

item.title: Cellular Automata in Earth Engine
item.description: Conway's Game of Life is cool. Earth Engine is cool. What if we put them together?

item.title: Optimizing Module Structure in Earth Engine
item.description: Nested file structures are great for organization and terrible for import speed in Earth Engine modules. Let's figure out why and what we can do about it.

item.title: Should You Minify Your Earth Engine Modules?
item.description: Websites minify code to make pages load faster, so can we speed up Earth Engine imports by minifying our modules? Nope, not really. It turns out there's more to import times than file size.

item.title: Dithering in Earth Engine
item.description: Dithering is an image processing technique used in retro video games to simulate shading with a single color. Let's apply it to Landsat imagery in Earth Engine.
